sourceMovie.title: One True Thing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: One True Thing (1998) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Carl Franklin's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Punch-Drunk Love
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If One True Thing clicked for you, Punch-Drunk Love (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Ultimate Gift
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If One True Thing clicked for you, The Ultimate Gift (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after One True Thing?
faq.answer: Try Punch-Drunk Love, The Ultimate Gift, Restless—each shares DNA with One True Thing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is One True Thing?
faq.answer: One True Thing (1998) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is One True Thing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
